name: AB 250/2 Italian Air Torpedo Variant
summary: Italian cluster bomb container carrying multiple small anti-personnel or incendiary submunitions. Used by the Regia Aeronautica in North Africa and the Mediterranean.

name: Barchina Esplosiva (MTM)
summary: A small plywood motorboat packed with 300kg of explosive. The pilot aimed at a target, locked the wheel, and bailed out before impact. Used in the raid on Souda Bay (1941) that damaged HMS York.

name: Gamma class frogman suit
summary: Italy pioneered combat diving with the Gamma Group (10th MAS). Operators wore rubberised suits, closed-circuit oxygen rebreathers, and fins — equipment that was revolutionary for the era and influenced post-war special forces globally.

operational_use: Alexandria (1941), Gibraltar (multiple raids), other Mediterranean ports
name: Siluro a Lenta Corsa (SLC) 'Maiale'
summary: Italy's most famous special weapon. Two frogmen rode astride a slow-running torpedo to enemy harbours, detached the warhead, and set a timer before making their escape. In December 1941, operators from the 10th MAS Flotilla sank HMS Queen Elizabeth and HMS Valiant at Alexandria, immobilising the British Mediterranean Battle Fleet.
